design_philosophy.core_loop: Side-scrolling hand-to-hand combat against wave-based enemy formations; toggle between close-range kunai attacks and limited-supply long-range shuriken projectiles
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management (shuriken ammo) balanced against melee engagement; wave attrition across six level progression
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as androids (Kunoichi or Ninja) executing a revolutionary mission against an oppressive government; ninja warrior combat mastery
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Commits fully to arcade beat-em-up simplicity without progression complexity; emphasizes visual spectacle (triple-wide screen in arcade) over narrative depth; treats story as backdrop for action rather than co-equal design pillar

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Choice between two characters with presumably distinct movesets; resource-based tension from limited shuriken supply creates self-imposed difficulty scaling

progression.unlock_mechanism: Six sequential levels; character selection at start (Kunoichi or Ninja)
replayability_hooks: Two distinct playable characters with different combat profiles
Wave-based level design encourages pattern recognition and optimization
Resource management (shuriken conservation) creates variable difficulty per playthrough

world_building_techniques: Dystopian setting (2093 martial law under President Banglar) established via manual/external documentation
Character codenames (Kunoichi, Ninja) and android identity reinforce sci-fi rebellion premise
Enemy faction (Banglar forces) serves as thematic antagonist rather than narratively developed opponent

faction_indicators: Banglar regime (authoritarian government enforcing martial law)
Android protagonists (external creation by scientists, not organic rebellion)
danger_signals: Martial law declaration across entire nation
Creation of synthetic soldiers as insurgent tool
environmental_storytelling: Six-level progression through Banglar-controlled territories (implied spatial geography of resistance journey)
Enemy wave design reflects organized military opposition rather than scattered resistance
Arcade cabinet's triple-wide screen metaphor emphasizes vast scope of conflict environment

sources_used: Wikipedia - The Ninja Warriors (1987 arcade original article, contains references to 1994 version)
